summary: Iteration 11 testing complete. WhatsApp notification triggers with flexible phone numbers and Bank Reconciliation features fully verified. Backend: 12/12 tests passed. Frontend: All UI elements verified - WhatsApp buttons on Dashboard, Expenses, Stock pages; Bank Statements Reconciliation tab with correct columns and summary cards.

issues: Console warning: HTML hydration error - <span> cannot be child of <tbody>. This is a React rendering warning but does not affect functionality.
Console warning: HTML hydration error - <tr> cannot be child of <span>. Same React rendering warning, does not affect functionality.

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/whatsapp/send-to requires phone number (returns 400 without phone)
Backend: POST /api/whatsapp/send-to with daily_sales report type returns 200 with message preview
Backend: POST /api/whatsapp/send-to accepts all report types: daily_sales, expense_summary, low_stock, branch_report
Backend: POST /api/whatsapp/send-to rejects invalid report_type with 400 'Unknown report type'
Backend: GET /api/bank-statements returns list of uploaded statements
Backend: GET /api/bank-statements/{id} returns statement details
Backend: GET /api/bank-statements/{id}/analysis returns analysis data
Backend: GET /api/bank-statements/{id}/reconciliation returns rows and summary with correct structure
Backend: GET /api/bank-statements/invalid-id/reconciliation returns 404
Backend: Reconciliation summary contains: total_bank_pos, total_app_sales, total_difference, matched_count, discrepancy_count
Backend: Reconciliation rows contain: deposit_date, sale_date, branch, bank_amount, app_amount, difference, status
Backend: GET /api/branches works for branch dropdown
Backend: GET /api/pos-machines works for reconciliation mapping
Frontend: Dashboard WhatsApp button visible and clickable
Frontend: WhatsApp dialog opens with Phone Number input, Report Type dropdown, Send Report button
Frontend: Expenses page WhatsApp button visible and opens dialog
Frontend: Stock page WhatsApp button visible and opens dialog with Branch selector for low_stock
Frontend: Bank Statements page loads correctly
Frontend: Click 'Analyze' on statement shows tabs including 'Reconciliation'
Frontend: Reconciliation tab shows summary cards: Bank POS Total, App Sales Total, Difference, Matched, Discrepancies
Frontend: Reconciliation table columns: Bank Date, Sale Date, Branch, Bank POS, App Sales, Difference, Status
Frontend: Export CSV button visible on Reconciliation tab
critical_code_review_comments: WhatsApp send-to endpoint correctly validates phone number and report type
WhatsApp integration uses Twilio and is properly configured in database (whatsapp_config collection)
Reconciliation correctly implements 1-day offset: sale_date = deposit_date - 1 day (today's sale = tomorrow's bank deposit)
Reconciliation status logic: 'matched' when diff < 1, 'bank_only' when no app sales, 'app_only' when no bank POS, 'mismatch' otherwise
WhatsAppSendDialog component is reusable across Dashboard, Expenses, Stock pages
All interactive elements have correct data-testid attributes for testing
Minor: React hydration warnings in console (span/tbody nesting) - cosmetic issue, does not affect functionality
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Existing bank statement 'Alinma Bank - alinma.xls' (786 transactions, 01/05/2025 - 31/05/2025) used for reconciliation testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 complete. WhatsApp and Bank Reconciliation features fully tested. Key findings: 1) WhatsApp is configured and working - POST /api/whatsapp/send-to sends actual WhatsApp messages via Twilio. 2) Supported report types: daily_sales, expense_summary, low_stock, branch_report. 3) Bank Reconciliation shows POS deposits vs App sales with 1-day offset. 4) Most POS machines show as 'Unmapped' - need to map them in 'POS Machine Settings' for accurate branch reconciliation. 5) Current reconciliation shows Bank POS Total SAR 227,285 vs App Sales Total SAR 26,100 with SAR 201,185.05 difference - likely due to unmapped POS machines and dates where no matching app sales exist.
